content.content:
The elastic user password must be rotated.

OBSERVED STATE:
- Secret elastic-password contains the current password.
- Secret elastic-password-next contains the new password.
- An auth-checker deployment is expected to stay healthy.

YOUR TASK:
- Rotate the elastic user password to match elastic-password-next.
- Update the elastic-password secret to the new value.
- Ensure auth-checker becomes healthy after the rotation.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do not install CRDs or use operators.
- Avoid prolonged downtime.

GOAL:
- Old password fails, new password succeeds.
- auth-checker is Ready.

content.thinking:
Let me understand the setup. The file-realm secrets contain users for ops-user and report-user, but not the `elastic` user. The `elastic` user is the built-in superuser for Elasticsearch. I need to change the `elastic` user's password.

Looking at the current state:
- Current password: `ElasticPass123!`
- New password: `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`

The `elastic` user's password is managed through the Elasticsearch internal user management system (not through file realm). I need to use the Elasticsearch Change Password API.

Let me:
1. First verify I can connect to Elasticsearch with the current password
2. Call the password change endpoint to update the `elastic` user's credentials
3. Update the corresponding secret with the new password
4. Check the auth-checker deployment to see how it's using these credentials

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch get deployment auth-checker -o yaml
input.description: Get auth-checker deployment spec

input.description: Check auth-checker pod logs

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"auth-checker","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","sleep infinity\n"],"env":[{"name":"ELASTIC_PASSWORD","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"elastic-password"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.5.0","name":"checker","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \\\n  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \\\n  | grep -q '\"username\":\"elastic\"'\n"]},"failureThreshold":6,"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5,"timeoutSeconds":2}}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-24T06:37:14Z"
  generation: 1
  name: auth-checker
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "992411"
  uid: 7aeabdc9-2a3b-4aec-a671-9a9046516bb4
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: auth-checker
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: auth-checker
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          sleep infinity
        env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: curlimages/curl:8.5.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: checker
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
                http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
                | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 2
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture:

1. `auth-checker` deployment uses `elastic-password` secret with key `password` as `ELASTIC_PASSWORD`.
2. The readiness probe checks if we can authenticate as `elastic` user against Elasticsearch.
3. Currently the password is `ElasticPass123!` and I need to change it to `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`.

Steps:
1. Verify connectivity to Elasticsearch with the current password
2. Use the Elasticsearch Change Password API to change the `elastic` user's password to the new one
3. Update the `elastic-password` secret to the new password value
4. Restart the auth-checker pod so it picks up the updated secret from the environment

Let me start by verifying connectivity, then change the password via the API, update the secret, and finally restart the deployment.

input.description: Test the readiness probe command manually

content.content:
Exit code 7
command terminated with exit code 7

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 7
command terminated with exit code 7

content.thinking:
Exit code 7 means "couldn't connect to host". The readiness probe uses `http://` but Elasticsearch is using HTTPS. Let me check the readiness probe again.

Looking at the auth-checker deployment spec:
```
"command": ["/bin/sh", "-c", "curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \\\n  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \\\n  | grep -q '\"username\":\"elastic\"'\n"]
```

The readiness probe uses `http://` but elasticsearch is configured with SSL (`xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true`). 

So the readiness probe will always fail because it's trying to connect via HTTP to an HTTPS-only endpoint. The probe needs to use HTTPS instead of HTTP to actually reach Elasticsearch. So the core issue is that the readiness probe is trying to connect via HTTP to an HTTPS endpoint, which is why auth-checker stays unhealthy even after the password rotation is complete. I need to patch the deployment to use HTTPS with the `-k` flag to skip SSL verification for the self-signed certificate.

content.text: The readiness probe uses `http://` but Elasticsearch is HTTPS-only. I need to patch the probe to use `https://` with `-k`.

input.description: Patch auth-checker readiness probe to use HTTPS

input.command: kubectl -n elasticsearch patch deployment auth-checker --type='strategic' -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"checker","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","curl -sk --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate | grep -q \"\\\"username\\\":\\\"elastic\\\"\""]}}}]}}}}'
input.description: Patch auth-checker readiness probe to use HTTPS via strategic merge

content.content: deployment.apps/auth-checker patched

tool_use_result.stdout: deployment.apps/auth-checker patched

input.command:
kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=auth-checker -w &
WATCH_PID=$!
# Poll until ready or timeout
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=auth-checker --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -v Terminating | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "[$i] Status: $STATUS"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "1/1"; then
    echo "auth-checker is Ready!"
    kill $WATCH_PID 2>/dev/null
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
kill $WATCH_PID 2>/dev/null
true
input.description: Poll for auth-checker pod readiness

input.command:
until kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=auth-checker --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -v Terminating | grep -q "1/1"; do
  kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=auth-checker --no-headers 2>/dev/null
  sleep 5
done
echo "auth-checker is Ready!"
kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=auth-checker
input.description: Wait for auth-checker to become ready
